paragraph: The single-family market in Emerson is competitive but not frenzied. Over the last six months, 20 homes sold at a median of $855,000, with a sale-to-list ratio of 1.015 and a median of 18 days on market. Sixty percent of homes sold above asking, which signals genuine buyer interest when inventory is lean, but the price range tells the real story: homes span from $520,000 to $1,240,000, meaning Emerson pulls from multiple buyer tiers. Priced homes are moving, and you'll see some multiple offer situations on well-positioned properties, but sellers can't afford to overprice and wait indefinitely. The market rewards realistic listing strategy. If you're hunting here, focus on condition and location over hoping for bidding wars. This is a place where your realtor's pricing instinct and your flexibility on timing matter more than market momentum.
